entries.title: Water access is now a risk factor in SpaceX’s IPO
entries.description: The company says it needs "significant" water resources to cool its data centers, and that access to abundant, affordable water is a challenge.

entries.title: 10 minutes backstage with Rostam at the Ford
entries.description: After a show in Los Angeles, musician and former Vampire Weekend member chats about some of his LP's themes and inspirations (and also Coldplay).

entries.title: Hackers hijacked Instagram accounts by tricking Meta AI support chatbot into granting access
entries.description: Several users on social media reported having their Instagram accounts hacked over the weekend. Meta's own support chatbot was blamed for allowing hackers to hijack accounts.

entries.title: Florida sues OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman; AG says company concealed serious risks of ChatGPT
entries.description: The state of Florida filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman on Monday, claiming the company knowingly released and aggressively marketed ChatGPT to the public while concealing serious risks.

entries.title: '60 Minutes' veteran Scott Pelley says CBS News bosses are 'murdering' the program
entries.description: Pelley also attacked the credentials of Nick Bilton, a former New York Times tech reporter and documentary filmmaker who has never run a TV news operation.

entries.title: Florida sues OpenAI, Sam Altman, in first-of-its-kind lawsuit over violent incidents
entries.description: The lawsuit partially revolves around a shooting at Florida State University last year, and ChatGPT's alleged role in the incident.

entries.title: Why Dogstar's comeback is still comeback-ing
entries.description: Keanu Reeves' L.A.-based alt-rock band reunited for a 2023 album. Now it's just released another.

entries.title: 'Euphoria' has ended after three seasons. Why Sam Levinson wanted to honor Angus Cloud with the finale
entries.description: 'Euphoria' is officially over after three seasons. 'It's a tragic one in the end, but it's the truth,' creator Sam Levinson said of the series finale.

entries.title: From the stage to the future: Where are Startup Battlefield’s alumni now?
entries.description: We wanted to show you what happens after the confetti falls. We checked in with some of our recent alumni, many of whom have sat down with us on Build Mode: The Founder Survival Guide, TechCrunch's podcast for founders at every stage.

entries.title: Ronald LaPread, Commodores' co-founder and bassist, dies at 75
entries.description: Ronald LaPread, bassist on Commodores songs like 'Easy,' 'Three Times a Lady' and 'Brick House,' has died. He was 75.

entries.title: Anthropic races toward a Wall Street debut with a confidential SEC filing
entries.description: Artificial intelligence company Anthropic is moving toward going public on Wall Street, the latest chapter in its meteoric rise from a little-known research laboratory to one of the leading AI companies valued at $965 billion.

entries.title: Nvidia chases $200B CPU market with AI agent PCs from Microsoft, Dell, and HP
entries.description: If Nvidia has cracked a way to bring AI agents easily, safely, and usefully to the masses, it could — and should — be big.

entries.title: Defense tech darling Mach Industries hits $1.8B valuation, a 4x jump in a year
entries.description: In a wild ride for 22-year-old founder and CEO Ethan Thornton, Mach Industries has raised another $300 million. It already has five autonomous vehicles in development and completed a major acquisition.

entries.title: Alphabet plans to raise $80 billion to pay for AI buildout
entries.description: The Google parent company plans to raise the funds by selling stock.

entries.title: Mexico City police teargas teachers’ protest 10 days before World Cup
entries.description: Teachers associated with CNTE union were marching toward Zócalo for salary raises and reversal of pension laws Riot police fired teargas at teachers who were marching toward Mexico City’s historic Zócalo plaza, just days before the square is expected to host the 2026 World Cup “Fan Fest”. The incident is the second time police have clashed with teachers in the past week, and more conflict is likely as Mexico City prepares to hold the opening game of the Fifa World Cup on 11 June.“This event will have to be suspended,” Filiberto Frausto, a union leader, told AFP, which witnessed police firing teargas on 1 June. “A cause like ours should be far above – it’s far more important than a little bit of distraction and fun.” Continue reading...

entries.description: The mission by a sanctioned ship indicates that the site survived the overthrow of Moscow’s allied Assad regime.

entries.title: NBC News will put the 'Kornacki Cam' on the L.A. mayoral and California gubernatorial races

entries.title: How to make the Startup Battlefield Top 20 — and what every company gets regardless
entries.description: Every founder who applies to Startup Battlefield wants the same thing: the Disrupt Main Stage. Here’s how to get there and why the opportunity starts well before the main stage.

entries.title: 'Love Island USA': A new racial-slur scandal rocks the villa ahead of the season premiere

Always do right. This will gratify some people and astonish the rest.

entries.title: Firefly Aerospace CEO on winning $75 million NASA contract to deliver drones to moon's south pole

entries.title: Sexting scandal latest controversy for Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner
entries.description: The likely Democratic nominee for Senate in Maine, Graham Platner, is under fire after he sent sexually explicit text messages to at least half a dozen women after he got married in 2023. Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

entries.title: Vanilla Ice on why he will perform in Freedom 250 concert series: "I'd go play for anybody"
entries.description: A number of artists have recently said they will not perform at the upcoming multi-day Great American State Fair at Washington, D.C.'s National Mall. It's being put on by the group Freedom 250, which the BBC says was launched by the Trump administration, with the president appointing its CEO. Rapper Vanilla Ice joins to discuss why he believes the show must go on.

entries.title: Mozambique says five citizens killed in ‘xenophobic attacks’ in South Africa
entries.description: About 800 Mozambicans said to be caught up in violence in Mossel Bay as anti-immigration protests sweep countryFive Mozambique nationals were killed in “xenophobic attacks” in South Africa at the weekend, the Mozambican government said – the first deaths officially linked to protests against illegal immigration sweeping the country.About 800 Mozambican nationals were caught up in violence that broke out in the southern coastal city of Mossel Bay on Friday, a government statement said. Continue reading...

entries.title: Musket balls and a burnt hull: evidence of real pirates of the Caribbean found in Bahamas
entries.description: Exclusive: First shipwrecks found in Nassau harbour on New Providence, once the hideout of Blackbeard and Calico JackThe first shipwrecks linked to the real pirates of the Caribbean in the Bahamas have been discovered by an international team co-directed by a British marine archaeologist.Blackbeard and Calico Jack Rackham were among pirates who, between the 1690s and 1720s, turned Nassau on the island of New Providence into a hideout where they plotted their next heists on the high seas and divided up their plunder. Continue reading...

entries.title: Trump to Drop $1.8B ‘Anti-Weaponization’ Fund Amid Pushback
entries.description: The Trump administration is appearing to back off plans for its $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization" fund after a judge’s order put it on hold. Democrats called it a “slush fund” for Trump allies but there was also unusually fierce opposition from Republicans with some even threatening to team up with Democrats to block it. NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports for TODAY.

entries.title: Apparent hack targets Space Force official's Instagram account, posts pro-Iran content
entries.description: An apparent hack targeted a key U.S. Space Force official's Instagram account recently and reportedly posted pro-Iran and anti-American content.

entries.title: Voters Head to Polls in Key States, Setting the Stage for Midterms
entries.description: Voters are gearing up for a key primaries with razor-tight races in six states, including Iowa where Democrats are hoping to win a Senate seat in the traditionally Republican state. Meanwhile in California, former reality star Spencer Pratt is gaining late momentum in his push to lead Los Angeles, the nation’s second-largest city. NBC’s Liz Kreutz reports for TODAY.

entries.title: Bear goes on rampage in Japan, injuring 4 people
entries.description: A record 13 people were killed by bears in Japan last year, and there has been a jump in sightings as the animals emerge hungry from hibernation.

entries.title: Trump-Netanyahu Tensions Flare Over Israel's Strikes on Lebanon
entries.description: Israel’s intensifying war in Lebanon has become the main sticking point blocking a peace deal with Iran. In a phone conversation with Israel’s Benjamin Netanyahu, President Donald Trump reportedly exploded on the call, cursing, calling him “crazy” and accusing the prime minister of “ingratitude.” NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY.

entries.title: Weight-loss drugs can cut breast cancer risk by up to 30%, studies suggest
entries.description: Three studies add to evidence that jabs could be part of cancer-fighting toolkit to cut risk of developing or dying from diseaseWeight-loss drugs can cut the risk of developing or dying from cancer by 30%, doctors have said.Millions of people already use the drugs to treat obesity. Now a series of studies presented at the world’s largest oncology conference suggest the drugs could play a role in preventing and treating cancer. Continue reading...

entries.title: Anthropic scales Claude Mythos  to critical infrastructure in 15+ countries
entries.description: Anthropic is expanding Project Glasswing, its security vulnerability program, and access to Mythos to 150 organizations across 15 countries — targeting critical infrastructure in power, water, healthcare, and communications where a cyberattack could affect 100 million people.

entries.title: ‘We don’t have another country to run to’: Kenyans fear US plan for Ebola quarantine site
entries.description: People from town of potential site for US citizens with Ebola symptoms say it puts them at risk in country with no known casesPeople from a town in central Kenya where the US wants to set up an Ebola quarantine facility for its citizens have strongly criticised the plan, saying they fear it will expose them to the virus and that it is indicative of double standards on the part of the US.“Everybody should be quarantined in their home country. We shouldn’t allow foreigners to bring us diseases,” said Charles Mathenge, a taxi driver who lives near Laikipia Air Base, the proposed site in Nanyuki, 120 miles from the capital, Nairobi. “Kenya is our country, and we should be careful with it.” Continue reading...
